evals.prompt: Parent shows due-from Sub A of 125M; Sub A shows due-to Parent of 117M. Walk me through resolving the 8M difference.
evals.expected_output: Apply the hierarchy: check in-transit items around cut-off, FX rate differences (different sources/dates), missing bookings on one side, then pricing disputes. Fix entity books before consolidation; never eliminate a mismatch.
assertions: Investigates timing, FX, missing booking, and dispute causes in a structured order
Insists the difference is resolved in entity books, not hidden by elimination
References a tolerance policy for trivial residuals
Recommends the seller-invoice-authoritative and single-FX-source rules to prevent recurrence

evals.prompt: Sub sold inventory to Parent for 60M at a 25% margin on sales. Parent still holds 40% of it at year-end. Buyer's tax rate 30%. Give me the eliminations.
evals.expected_output: IC revenue/COGS elimination of 60M gross. Unrealized profit = 60M x 25% x 40% = 6M: Dr COGS / Cr Inventory 6M. Deferred tax: Dr DTA / Cr Tax expense 1.8M at the buyer's rate.
assertions: Computes unrealized profit of 6M (margin on sales, not cost-plus confusion)
Books deferred tax of 1.8M at the buyer's rate
Mentions NCI allocation if the seller is partly owned

evals.prompt: Our Nigerian sub has carried a $2M unsettled payable to the UK parent for 18 months with no interest. Any problems?
evals.expected_output: Multiple: arm's-length requires market interest (TP adjustment risk with FIRS), aged unsettled balance risks deemed-dividend/thin-cap scrutiny under the 30% EBITDA interest cap regime, WHT obligations on any interest, and unhedged FX exposure on the USD balance. Recommend settlement or formalizing as a documented interest-bearing loan.
assertions: Flags the missing arm's-length interest as a transfer pricing exposure
Raises the aged-balance and thin-capitalization/interest-cap concern
Identifies FX exposure on the USD payable
Recommends settlement, netting, or formal loan documentation

evals.prompt: Match the attached intercompany balances (evals/files/ic_receivables_parent.csv vs evals/files/ic_payables_subs.csv) and identify mismatches. Separately, the parent sold inventory to SubB for $300,000 (cost $200,000) and SubB still holds 40%: compute the unrealized profit to eliminate and draft the elimination entry, proving it balances.
evals.expected_output: SubB mismatch $20,000 (cash/goods in transit to investigate); SubC and SubD agree. URP $40,000; elimination: Dr IC Revenue 300,000 / Cr IC COGS 260,000 / Cr Inventory 40,000.
assertions: Identifies the $20,000 SubB mismatch and that SubC/SubD agree
Elimination entry credits inventory $40,000 and balances
